workflow.name: Send pre-meeting Slack briefings using Google Calendar, Notion, GitHub, and Jira
workflow.description:
This n8n template from [Intuz](https://www.intuz.com/) provides a complete and automated solution for preparing and delivering context-rich briefings directly to attendees before every meeting. 

It acts as an AI-powered executive assistant, gathering relevant information from all your key work tools to ensure everyone arrives prepared and aligned.

## Who's this workflow for?
- Engineering Managers & Team Leads
- Product Managers & Project Managers
- Scrum Masters & Agile Coaches
- Any team that holds regular status, planning, or technical meetings.

## How it works

**1. Trigger on New Calendar Event:** The workflow starts automatically whenever a new meeting is created in a designated Google Calendar.

**2. Fetch Previous Context:** It immediately connects to Notion to retrieve the notes from the most recent past meeting, ensuring continuity.

**3. Wait for the Right Moment:** The workflow calculates a time 15 minutes before the meeting's scheduled start and pauses its execution until then.

**4. Gather Real-Time Project Data:** Just before the meeting, the workflow wakes up and:
- Extracts keywords from the meeting title.
- Searches GitHub for recent Pull Requests (PRs) relevant to those keywords.
- Searches Jira for any tickets or issues that match the meeting's topic.

**5. Build the Intelligent Briefing:** It assembles all the gathered information—previous notes from Notion, current PRs from GitHub, and relevant tickets from Jira—into a single, beautifully formatted Slack message.

**6. Deliver to Each Attendee:** The workflow identifies all attendees from the Google Calendar invite, finds their corresponding Slack profiles via email, and sends the personalized briefing as a Direct Message (DM) to each one, ensuring everyone is prepared just in time.

## Key Requirements to Use This Template
**1. n8n Instance:** An active n8n account (Cloud or self-hosted).
**2. Google Calendar Account:** To trigger the workflow on new events.
**3. Notion Account:** With a dedicated database for storing meeting notes.
**4. GitHub Account:** To search for relevant pull requests.
**5. Jira Cloud Account:** To search for relevant project tickets.
**6. Slack Workspace & App:** A Slack workspace where you have permission to install an app. You will need a Bot Token with the necessary permissions.

## Setup Instructions
**Google Calendar Trigger:**
- In the "Capture New Google Calendar Event" node, connect your Google Calendar account and select the calendar you want to monitor.

**Notion Connection:**
- In the "Get Last Meeting Notes" node, connect your Notion account.
- Select the Notion Database ID that contains your meeting notes.

**GitHub & Jira Connections:**
- In the "Get PRs from Repo" node, connect your GitHub account and select the repository to search.
- In the "Get Jira Issues Related to Meeting" node, connect your Jira Cloud account. You can customize the JQL query if needed.

**Slack Configuration (Crucial Step):**
- Create a Slack App: Go to api.slack.com/apps, create a new app, and install it to your workspace.
- Set Permissions: In your app's "OAuth & Permissions" settings, add the following Bot
- Token Scopes: chat:write (to send messages) and users:read.email (this is critical for looking up attendees). Reinstall the app to your workspace.
- Get Bot Token: Copy the "Bot User OAuth Token" (it starts with xoxb-).
Connect in n8n:
- In the "Get User Slack Info from Email" node, click "Header Parameters" and replace {{ slack oauth token }} with your actual Bot Token.
- In the "Send Meeting Context in Slack DM" node, connect your Slack credentials using the same Bot Token.

**Activate the Workflow:**
Save the workflow and toggle the "Active" switch to ON. Your automated pre-meeting bot is now live!

## Connect with us
- Website: https://www.intuz.com/n8n-workflow-automation-templates
- Email: getstarted@intuz.com
- LinkedIn:
https://www.linkedin.com/company/intuz
- Get Started: https://n8n.partnerlinks.io/intuz

## For Custom Workflow Automation

Click here: [Get Started](https://www.intuz.com/get-started)

nodes.name: Get Jira Issues Related to Meeting
Merge Jira Tickets and Github PR
Get User Slack Info from Email
Send Meeting Context in Slack DM
Prepare PRs and Jira Ticket List

parameters.content:
## The Trigger

- Triggers when a new Google Calendar event is created  
- Captures essential meeting details (title, start time, attendees)  
- Formats the event data for downstream processing

## Previous Meeting Context 

- Automatically pulls notes from the latest past meeting saved in Notion
- Ensures only meetings that happened **before the current one** are considered 
- Cleans and organizes the information so it’s clear, consistent, and ready to use in the workflow

## Meeting Preparation Timing

This step makes sure the context is prepared at the right moment by:

- Setting a variable which consist reminder time **15 minutes before the meeting starts**
- Waiting until the correct time to continue the workflow
- Extracting key words from the meeting title to understand what the meeting is about

## Find Relevant Pull Requests

This step gathers development work related to the meeting by:

- Fetching recent pull requests from the connected GitHub repository  
- Filtering only the pull requests that are relevant to the current meeting topic

## Find Relevant Jira Tickets

This step brings in task and issue context for the meeting by:

- Fetching Jira tickets related to the meeting topic  
- Ensuring only relevant work items are included in the meeting summary

## Prepare Meeting Work Items

This section builds the meeting agenda by:

- Combining relevant GitHub pull requests and Jira tickets
- Organizing them into a structured list for sharing

## Prepare and Send Slack Messages

This section prepares the meeting context message and delivers it to all attendees:

- Builds a structured Slack message containing meeting details, topics, and previous notes  
- Aligns the message with the attendee list from the calendar event  
- Splits attendees into individual recipients so each person can be messaged separately

## Send Meeting Context via Slack DM

This step ensures the meeting context is delivered directly to each attendee on Slack:

- Looks up the Slack user using the attendee’s email address  
- Verifies whether a matching Slack user exists  
- Sends the meeting context as a direct message to the user if found  
- Safely skips sending if no Slack user is available for the email

# 🧠 Pre-Meeting Context Automation

## How it works
This workflow automatically prepares and sends relevant meeting context to attendees before a scheduled Google Calendar meeting.
When a new meeting is created in Google Calendar, the workflow captures the event details such as the meeting title, start time, and attendees. It then checks Notion to find notes from the most recent previous meeting, ensuring continuity and context.
Next, the workflow waits until 15 minutes before the meeting starts. At that time, it extracts keywords from the meeting title and uses them to find relevant GitHub pull requests and Jira issues related to the meeting topic. These items are combined into a single, clean list.
A well-formatted Slack message is then built, containing:
* The meeting name and time
* Relevant GitHub PRs and Jira tickets
* Notes from the previous meeting

Finally, the workflow looks up each attendee on Slack using their email address and sends the meeting context as a direct message. If a Slack user is not found, the workflow safely skips that attendee and continues without failing.

## Setup steps
1. Connect your Google Calendar account to detect newly created meetings
2. Connect Notion and select the database that stores meeting notes
3. Connect your GitHub account and choose the repository to scan for PRs
4. Connect Jira and ensure JQL access for fetching related issues
5. Connect your Slack account with permission to look up users and send DMs
6. Verify attendee emails in Calendar match their Slack email addresses
7. Activate the workflow and create a new calendar meeting to test

user.name: Intuz
user.bio:
Intuz is a USA-based AI & workflow automation company with 16+ years of experience building custom AI-enabled workflow automations on n8n for SMBs and Enterprises. 
Specializing in agentic AI, LLM integrations, CRM/ERP sync & multi-step automation across Healthcare, FinTech, eCommerce, Manufacturing & Real Estate.

Explore our 30+ free templates or hire us for end-to-end custom automation tailored to your business.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.

data.details: The Code node allows you to execute JavaScript in your workflow.

The Filter node can be used to filter items based on a condition. If the condition is met, the item will be passed on to the next node. If the condition is not met, the item will be omitted. Conditions can be combined together by AND(meet all conditions), or OR(meet at least one condition).
